entries.statement: Knowledge of data valuation and infonomics methodologies, including frameworks for quantifying data as an enterprise asset and connecting data investment to measurable financial outcomes.

Knowledge of organizational change management principles as applied to large-scale data and AI transformation programs, including adoption barriers, culture change levers, and executive sponsorship requirements.
Knowledge of enterprise transformation program design, including operating model change, capability maturity progression, transformation roadmap construction, and change sequencing principles.
Knowledge of data and AI organizational design patterns, including centralized CoE, federated, and hub-and-spoke models, and the conditions under which each is appropriate.
Knowledge of innovation program design, including ideation methods, prototype development approaches, value hypothesis construction, and stage-gate evaluation criteria.
Knowledge of data and AI maturity assessment frameworks and how to diagnose organizational capability gaps across strategy, governance, architecture, talent, and culture dimensions.
Knowledge of data and AI business case construction, including value driver identification, benefit quantification methods, investment sizing, and risk-adjusted return analysis.

Knowledge of data and AI strategy development approaches, including strategy formulation methods, alignment with enterprise business strategy, competitive positioning through data capabilities, and strategy communication frameworks.

Knowledge of talent management practices for data and AI organizations, including workforce planning methodologies, skills gap analysis, hiring strategy, retention approaches, career path development, and succession planning.

Knowledge of data and AI governance structures at the board and executive committee level, including oversight responsibilities, reporting cadences, decision rights allocation, and escalation frameworks.

Knowledge of the data and AI vendor ecosystem, including major platform providers, emerging technology categories, vendor lock-in risks, open-source alternatives, and strategic partnership evaluation criteria.

Knowledge of data and AI due diligence frameworks for mergers, acquisitions, and private equity transactions, including data asset valuation, technical debt assessment, key person risk evaluation, and integration complexity analysis.
Knowledge of data monetization models and data product strategy, including internal data products, external data licensing, data-enabled services, and data marketplace design principles.

Knowledge of executive-level AI risk governance structures and processes, including board and C-suite accountability mechanisms, AI risk committee design, risk escalation protocols, and governance role definitions.

Knowledge of AI testing, evaluation, verification, and validation (TEVV) frameworks and incident management infrastructure, including designing test environments, establishing performance assessment protocols, and developing AI incident response procedures.

Knowledge of AI supply chain risk management, including assessment of third-party AI component risks, intellectual property considerations, vendor dependency analysis, and contingency planning for external AI dependencies.

Knowledge of AI privacy risk governance, including privacy impact assessments, data minimization principles applied to AI systems, algorithmic impact assessments, and privacy-preserving AI design considerations.

Knowledge of building and managing diverse AI risk management teams, including cross-disciplinary expertise requirements, stakeholder engagement strategies, inclusion of domain experts, and team composition principles.

Knowledge of human-AI interaction governance, including defining human oversight roles for AI systems, establishing human-in-the-loop decision frameworks, and designing organizational controls for AI system autonomy.

Knowledge of data storytelling and narrative frameworks for presenting data insights to leadership, including visual communication principles, compelling narrative construction, and methods for translating analytical findings into actionable business recommendations.

Knowledge of organizational data culture and cultural transformation strategies, including identifying cultural barriers to data adoption, leveraging cultural change levers, and promoting organizational alignment around data-driven decision-making.

Knowledge of AI workforce planning and recruitment strategies, including identifying AI skill requirements, developing recruitment pipelines for specialized AI talent, addressing talent retention challenges, and building sustainable AI workforce capacity.

Knowledge of AI resource allocation and alternative analysis methodologies, including assessing resource requirements for AI initiatives, conducting cost-benefit analysis across competing AI projects, and optimizing resource utilization.

Knowledge of AI system business value evaluation and benefit realization frameworks, including assessing business use cases for AI systems, defining success metrics, and evaluating actual benefits against planned outcomes.

Knowledge of competitive intelligence and market analysis methodologies for data and AI, including analyzing competitive data and AI capabilities, identifying market opportunities through data assets, and positioning organizational data strategy in competitive context.

Knowledge of data-driven decision-making frameworks at the strategic level, including methodologies for quantifying risk in strategic decisions, measuring decision impact, and developing decision governance processes.

Knowledge of data and AI portfolio management, including portfolio prioritization frameworks, balancing innovation versus operational efficiency investments, managing interdependencies across initiatives, and aligning portfolio strategy with business strategy.

Knowledge of emerging AI trends and use cases, including monitoring technological advancements, assessing applicability of new AI capabilities to organizational context, and planning for adoption of emerging AI techniques.
